title: Docs Viewer
summary: Entry point for using, operating, and changing the shared Docs Viewer reader and its local management and review surfaces.
content_html:
Docs Viewer

Docs Viewer turns Markdown collections into navigable documentation sites. It provides the shared reader used by public routes, the local management route, and validated-package review.

This page is a task router. Overview gives the short capabilities and architecture model.

Use Docs Viewer

  • Docs Import — turn staged HTML, Markdown, documents, images, and reviewed collections into source docs.
  • Docs Review — inspect a validated returned package before choosing whether to import it.
  • Export — create a standalone HTML copy from generated local-scope payloads.
  • Media Handling — understand imported images, attachments, storage, and publication.

Maintain Docs And Scopes

  • Source Organisation — source roots, front matter, hierarchy, and generated ordering.
  • Document Identity — immutable document codes, date provenance, and create-versus-update rules.
  • Builder — build and publish generated document and search payloads.
  • Scope Lifecycle — create, rename, and delete configured scopes through previewed local-service plans.
  • Portability Snapshot — inactive record of earlier portability thinking; revisit only if it becomes real work.
  • Testing — choose the appropriate repository check.

Change The Architecture

  • Configuration And Extension Points — what drives routes, scopes, reports, views, actions, imports, and service availability.
  • Runtime Architecture — public, manage, and review execution paths, authority layers, extension method, and weak spots.
  • Generated Data Contracts — generated payloads, read authority, and publishing.
  • Runtime Module Ownership — grouped browser-module owners when a code change needs exact responsibility.
  • Source Tree Ownership — repository-level source, runtime, config, service, and generated-output boundaries.

Code Authority

Use documentation to find the relevant boundary, then verify exact behaviour in:

  • docs-viewer/config/routes/ and docs-viewer/config/scopes/ for routes, available features, scopes, and output locations
  • site/docs-viewer/runtime/js/shared/ for public-safe reader primitives
  • docs-viewer/runtime/js/ for manage-, review-, import-, and report-owned browser code
  • docs-viewer/services/ for local reads, writes, imports, exports, review, and scope workflows
  • docs-viewer/build/ and docs-viewer/tests/ for generated contracts and executable evidence

Prefer code/config search for exact modules, endpoints, fields, and files. Copying those lists into overview pages makes them harder to trust.

Plan Docs Viewer Work

  • Docs Viewer Roadmap — current importance, dependencies, features, and finishable outcomes.
  • Roadmap — the shared app-roadmap, feature, concept, architecture, and delivery model.
